name: Pine64 Star64
description: Pine64's first RISC-V SBC, built on the StarFive JH7110 quad-core SiFive U74 SoC with Imagination BXE-4-32 GPU; positioned as an open-platform RISC-V learning/development board.

release-date.comment: Public availability began April 4, 2023; product was announced earlier (August 2022) but production slipped.

status.comment: Both 4GB and 8GB Model-A variants listed as 'In stock' on the Pine64 store as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: Pine64 publishes schematics for many boards but does not release PCB/Gerber design files; not formally OSHW-licensed. Schematic v1.1 (dated 20230504) is publicly available for the Star64.

country-of-origin.comment: Pine64 is headquartered in Fremont, California; Pine Store Limited is incorporated in Hong Kong; boards ship from Shenzhen warehouses.

cpu-cores.comment: Quad-core SiFive U74 application cores. The JH7110 also includes an S7 monitor core and an E24 real-time control core, but the user-visible Linux core count is 4.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Vendor-stated 'up to 1.5 GHz' for the U74 cluster.

process-node.comment: StarFive does not publicly disclose the JH7110 process node; commonly reported as 28 nm in the community but not officially confirmed by primary sources, so left null per Assessment Guidelines.

vulkan-support.comment: Hardware supports Vulkan 1.2 (vendor BXE driver). Open-source/mainline driver maturity is still early; Vulkan availability in distro images is patchy.

hw-video-decode.comment: JH7110 video decoder supports H.264/AVC and H.265/HEVC up to 4Kp60. No VP9 or AV1.

hw-video-encode.comment: Hardware H.265 encode up to 1080p30 per CNX-Software's spec sheet; H.264 encode is not advertised by Pine64 for the JH7110.

ram-min-gb.comment: Pine64 store currently sells only 4 GB and 8 GB Model-A variants. The wiki mentions a 2 GB variant in the spec sheet, but it is not offered for sale; using 4 GB as the smallest configuration actually sold.

emmc.comment: Optional eMMC module socket (Pine64-style daughter card, up to 128 GB) — not soldered onboard.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No native M.2 slot. PCIe 2.0 x1 is exposed via a standard PCIe slot connector instead; users add NVMe via a PCIe-to-M.2 adapter.

sata.comment: No native SATA. Pine64 sells an optional PCIe-to-SATA adapter that uses the PCIe slot.

dual-ethernet.comment: Pine64 offers Single or Dual 10/100/1000 Mbps Ethernet variants; the production Model-A as sold ships with 2× Gigabit RJ45.

wifi.comment: Production Model-A boards use the Fn-Link RTL8852BU module (Realtek RTL8852BU), which is 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6) + BT 5.2. Older Pine64 wiki spec sheet still lists '802.11 b/g/n/ac' but contradicts the current product page; the chipset is the authoritative signal.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: No USB-C ports on the Star64; power input is a 5.5 mm DC barrel jack.

poe-support.comment: No PoE support, no PoE HAT path advertised by Pine64.

max-display-resolution.comment: Vendor lists 'up to 4K@30Hz' over HDMI; '4K HDR @ 60fps' is mentioned on the wiki but applies to the SoC display pipeline / HDR signaling rather than a sustained 60 Hz desktop mode.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Pine64 'Pi2' header is mechanically and electrically compatible with the Raspberry Pi 40-pin GPIO layout for most pins, but software pin-mapping is RISC-V/JH7110-specific so HAT drivers may need porting.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Mechanical 40-pin compatibility is partial: the board is 133×80 mm (not RPi-standard 85×56 mm) and mounting holes do not match RPi HAT spec, so most HATs will not seat or align properly even though the header pinout is similar.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× PCIe 2.0 (x1 lane in mechanical x4 slot)

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I2C, SPI, UART all available on the GPIO header.

idle-power-w.comment: No credible measured idle-power figure found from Jeff Geerling, CNX-Software, Phoronix, or Pine64. Per RESEARCH.md notes #8, leaving null rather than guessing from TDP.

load-power-w.comment: No credible measured full-load figure found from independent reviewers; left null rather than estimated.

cooling-required.comment: Pine64 recommends a heatsink for sustained loads (especially in the NAS Case context); active cooling is optional. JH7110 is comparable to RPi 4 thermal class — heatsink-recommended is the most defensible bucket.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Pine64 documentation and product pages do not explicitly mention an RTC battery header for the Star64. Left null pending confirmation from the schematic; not safe to assume either direction.

form-factor-class.comment: 133×80 mm is well outside the ~85×56 mm Pi-standard envelope. Closer in spirit to a Quartz64-style Pine64 form factor; flagged as 'custom' per RESEARCH.md guidelines.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Mounting holes follow Pine64's own pattern (shared with Quartz64 / NAS Case), not the RPi standard.

weight-g.comment: Pine64 store lists shipping weight as 0.128 kg; bare-board weight may be slightly lower but no separate figure is published.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Star64 boots a stock upstream kernel (DTS upstreamed; Linux 6.14 added explicit Star64 support and U-Boot v2025.10 supports it natively, sharing binaries with VisionFive 2). However, GPU (BXE-4-32) and VPU acceleration still rely on out-of-tree Imagination/StarFive drivers, so this is 'partial' rather than 'full'.

official-os-image.comment: Pine64 itself does not publish a maintained first-party Linux image. They redirect users to community/third-party builds (Armbian, DietPi, Fishwaldo's Yocto images, NixOS, NuttX). Vendor docs explicitly note releases are 'still in alpha state, only fit for testing'.

distros-with-images.comment: Per RESEARCH.md, only counting distros that publish a board-specific image: Armbian (board page exists) and DietPi (Star64-specific Linux 6.1 image). Yocto/Fishwaldo and NixOS exist but are not in the defined tag set; community Debian/Ubuntu rootfs images run on Armbian's kernel but no upstream Debian/Ubuntu Star64-specific image is published.

bootloader.comment: OpenSBI (M-mode firmware) chained into mainline U-Boot (S-mode bootloader) as of v2025.10; shares binaries with the StarFive VisionFive 2.

long-term-support.comment: Pine64 has not committed to a stated software-support window. Software is community-driven and explicitly described as 'alpha' by Pine64; longevity depends on JH7110 mainline trajectory more than on Pine64 itself.

android-support.comment: No official or maintained Android image for the Star64; Pine64 software docs list only Linux distros and NuttX RTOS.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Pine64 store price for the 4 GB Model-A as of 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Pine64 store price for the 8 GB Model-A as of 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold globally via the Pine64 store (ships from Shenzhen/Hong Kong); also via resellers like ameriDroid (North America).

typical-lead-time.comment: Both 4 GB and 8 GB variants showed 'In stock' on the Pine64 store on 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate: Pine64 publishes a wiki, schematics, datasheet links, and a documentation portal with pinout and software notes, but coverage is uneven (e.g., no power measurements, no clear OS-image status) and the wiki spec block is partly stale (Wi-Fi 5 vs Wi-Fi 6 mismatch).

community-size.comment: Pine64 has a sizeable overall community, but the Star64 sub-community specifically is small relative to RPi/Rockchip; activity is concentrated in the dedicated Pine64 forum and a few RISC-V enthusiasts.

active-forum-discord.comment: Pine64 runs an active forum with a dedicated Star64 sub-board, plus the main Pine64 chat/Discord.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Limited: Pine64 sells a NAS Case, PCIe-to-SATA adapter, eMMC modules, and a few accessories. No vibrant third-party HAT/case ecosystem akin to RPi or Rockchip boards.
